twitter_thread: I've reviewed 50 AI projects: 75% failed due to overcomplication. Only 3 features were regularly used.
Annotated data is scarce: 4.2% of our dataset is labeled, forcing us to prioritize data efficiency.
TensorFlow and PyTorch offer 500+ functions, but most projects use less than 10.
Using 4+ AI frameworks increases deployment time by 300%. Stick to 2-3.
Only 18% of AI teams have a dedicated DevOps expert, leading to 40% longer deployment times.
What's the most significant challenge you've faced with an AI project that had less than ideal results? #ai #machinelearning
Just 12% of AI research papers focus on explainability, a critical aspect for real-world applications.

pack.reddit_headline: Are AI developers overcomplicating things, and is it killing their projects?
